entries.text: Prediction market FM-001 asks: "Will the Federal Reserve cut interest rates by 50 basis points at the September 2024 FOMC meeting?". This market belongs to the macro category. The market was triggered by FOMC Federal Reserve rate decision September 2024 on 2024-09-15. Odds moved from 0.25 to 0.55 (a +120.0% change). The market resolved YES on 2024-09-18. Fed Chair Jerome Powell signalled an outsized 50bp cut was on the table after August CPI came in below expectations at 2.5%. The Federal Reserve delivered the cut unanimously, marking its first rate reduction in four years. Treasury yields dropped 15bps immediately on the announcement.

Prediction market FM-002 asks: "Will the Federal Reserve cut interest rates at the November 2024 FOMC meeting?". This market belongs to the macro category. The market was triggered by FOMC Federal Reserve rate decision November 2024 on 2024-11-01. Odds moved from 0.68 to 0.91 (a +33.8% change). The market resolved YES on 2024-11-07. After the September 2024 rate cut, markets expected the Federal Reserve to continue its easing cycle at the November meeting. Strong jobs data briefly threatened the path but the FOMC delivered a 25bp cut as forecast. Fed officials cited continued disinflation progress and a softening labour market.

Prediction market FM-003 asks: "Will the Federal Reserve pause rate cuts at the January 2025 FOMC meeting?". This market belongs to the macro category. The market was triggered by FOMC Federal Reserve rate decision January 2025 on 2025-01-15. Odds moved from 0.38 to 0.82 (a +115.8% change). The market resolved YES on 2025-01-29. Sticky services inflation and a resilient jobs market prompted the Federal Reserve to hold rates in January 2025, pausing after three consecutive cuts. The FOMC statement removed language about further 'adjustments' being appropriate. Fed Chair Jerome Powell emphasised a data-dependent approach for the remainder of 2025.

Prediction market FM-004 asks: "Will the Federal Reserve cut interest rates at the December 2024 FOMC meeting?". This market belongs to the macro category. The market was triggered by FOMC Federal Reserve rate decision December 2024 on 2024-12-10. Odds moved from 0.7 to 0.86 (a +22.9% change). The market resolved YES on 2024-12-18. The Federal Reserve completed a third consecutive rate cut in December 2024, lowering the federal funds rate by 25bp to 4.25-4.50%. The FOMC signalled a slower pace of easing heading into 2025, projecting only two cuts for the following year. Markets had priced approximately 80% odds of the cut prior to the meeting.

Prediction market FM-005 asks: "Will the Federal Reserve cut interest rates at the June 2026 FOMC meeting?". This market belongs to the macro category. The market was triggered by FOMC Federal Reserve rate decision June 2026 on 2026-06-10. Odds moved from 0.48 to 0.55 (a +14.6% change). The market outcome is currently pending. With the federal funds rate at 4.00-4.25% after two cuts in 2025, markets are split on whether the Federal Reserve will ease further in June 2026. Persistent core PCE inflation above 2.5% and a tight labour market are pulling against rate cuts, while slowing GDP growth argues for easing.

Prediction market EL-001 asks: "Will Donald Trump win the 2024 US Presidential election?". This market belongs to the elections category. The market was triggered by 2024 US Presidential election outcome on 2024-11-05. Odds moved from 0.44 to 0.68 (a +54.5% change). The market resolved YES on 2024-11-06. Donald Trump defeated Democratic candidate Kamala Harris in the 2024 US Presidential election, winning key battleground states including Pennsylvania, Michigan, and Wisconsin. Trump's victory was called within 24 hours of polls closing. The Republican party simultaneously won control of the Senate, giving Trump a unified government.

Prediction market EL-002 asks: "Will Republicans win a majority in the US Senate in the 2024 elections?". This market belongs to the elections category. The market was triggered by 2024 US Senate election outcome on 2024-11-05. Odds moved from 0.55 to 0.88 (a +60.0% change). The market resolved YES on 2024-11-06. Republicans flipped Democrat-held Senate seats in West Virginia, Montana, and Ohio in the 2024 elections, securing a 53-47 majority. The Senate flip came alongside Donald Trump's presidential win. The unified Republican government opened the path for major legislative agenda items including tax cuts and deregulation.

Prediction market EL-003 asks: "Will the US government enter a shutdown in the first quarter of 2025?". This market belongs to the elections category. The market was triggered by US federal government budget deadline March 2025 on 2025-03-10. Odds moved from 0.28 to 0.58 (a +107.1% change). The market resolved YES on 2025-03-14. Congressional dysfunction over spending cuts triggered a brief government shutdown in mid-March 2025 lasting four days. Hardline Republicans demanded deeper discretionary cuts while Senate moderates balked. The shutdown ended when President Trump pressured House Republicans into accepting a bipartisan continuing resolution.

Prediction market CR-001 asks: "Will Bitcoin (BTC) reach $100,000 by December 31, 2024?". This market belongs to the crypto category. The market was triggered by Bitcoin BTC price rally post-election 2024 on 2024-11-20. Odds moved from 0.33 to 0.74 (a +124.2% change). The market resolved YES on 2024-12-05. Bitcoin crossed the $100,000 milestone on December 5, 2024, driven by Donald Trump's election victory and expectations of a crypto-friendly regulatory environment. The approval of spot Bitcoin ETFs in January 2024 earlier in the year had created sustained institutional demand. Coinbase and MicroStrategy both hit multi-year highs alongside the BTC rally.

Prediction market CR-002 asks: "Will a spot Bitcoin ETF be approved by the SEC in January 2024?". This market belongs to the crypto category. The market was triggered by SEC ruling on Bitcoin spot ETF applications on 2024-01-05. Odds moved from 0.55 to 0.93 (a +69.1% change). The market resolved YES on 2024-01-10. The SEC approved eleven spot Bitcoin ETF applications simultaneously on January 10, 2024, including products from BlackRock, Fidelity, and Invesco. The approval represented a landmark moment for Bitcoin adoption by institutional investors. Over $4 billion in net inflows entered spot Bitcoin ETF products within the first week of trading.

Prediction market CR-003 asks: "Will Bitcoin (BTC) fall below $50,000 at any point in Q2 2025?". This market belongs to the crypto category. The market was triggered by Bitcoin BTC price correction Q2 2025 on 2025-04-10. Odds moved from 0.22 to 0.6 (a +172.7% change). The market resolved NO on 2025-06-30. Despite a significant BTC sell-off in April 2025 that brought prices to $58,000, Bitcoin held above $50,000 throughout Q2 2025. Continued institutional buying through spot ETFs provided a floor. The market resolved NO as Bitcoin ended Q2 2025 around $72,000.

Prediction market CR-004 asks: "Will Ethereum (ETH) reach $5,000 at any point in 2025?". This market belongs to the crypto category. The market was triggered by Ethereum ETH price rally and ETF demand 2025 on 2025-06-01. Odds moved from 0.44 to 0.21 (a -52.3% change). The market resolved NO on 2025-12-31. Ethereum underperformed Bitcoin significantly in 2025, failing to reclaim its 2021 all-time high. ETH peaked near $3,900 in Q1 2025 before correcting. Despite spot Ethereum ETF approvals, net inflows were weak compared to spot Bitcoin ETFs. The market resolved NO with ETH ending 2025 at approximately $3,200.

Prediction market SP-001 asks: "Will the Kansas City Chiefs win Super Bowl LIX in February 2025?". This market belongs to the sports category. The market was triggered by Super Bowl LIX matchup Kansas City Chiefs vs Philadelphia Eagles on 2025-02-05. Odds moved from 0.42 to 0.57 (a +35.7% change). The market resolved NO on 2025-02-09. The Philadelphia Eagles defeated the Kansas City Chiefs 40-22 in Super Bowl LIX, ending Kansas City's bid for a third consecutive Super Bowl championship. Eagles quarterback Jalen Hurts threw for 286 yards and two touchdowns. The result was one of the most lopsided Super Bowl outcomes in recent memory.

Prediction market SP-002 asks: "Will Argentina win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?". This market belongs to the sports category. The market was triggered by 2026 FIFA World Cup group stage results Argentina on 2026-06-15. Odds moved from 0.17 to 0.26 (a +52.9% change). The market outcome is currently pending. Argentina, led by Lionel Messi in what is expected to be his final World Cup, advanced to the knockout rounds with three wins in the group stage. The defending champions are co-hosted by the US, Canada, and Mexico. Argentina faces Brazil in the quarter-finals, a fixture that has significantly moved prediction market odds.

Prediction market SP-003 asks: "Will Lionel Messi score at least one goal in the 2026 FIFA World Cup group stage?". This market belongs to the sports category. The market was triggered by Argentina vs Canada 2026 FIFA World Cup opener on 2026-06-22. Odds moved from 0.58 to 0.75 (a +29.3% change). The market resolved YES on 2026-06-22. Lionel Messi scored a trademark free kick in Argentina's 3-0 victory over Canada in the group stage opener. The goal was Messi's 100th in international competition. Prediction markets had opened at 58% prior to the match and shifted sharply once it became clear Messi was fit and starting.

Prediction market MA-001 asks: "Will the US economy enter a recession in 2025 as defined by two consecutive quarters of negative GDP growth?". This market belongs to the macro category. The market was triggered by US Q1 2025 GDP advance estimate release on 2025-04-30. Odds moved from 0.34 to 0.16 (a -52.9% change). The market resolved NO on 2025-12-31. The Q1 2025 GDP advance estimate showed growth of 1.8% annualised, above the recession threshold despite tariff headwinds. Consumer spending held firm and the labour market remained tight throughout 2025. The US economy avoided a technical recession even as GDP growth slowed from 2024's pace.

Prediction market MA-002 asks: "Will NVIDIA's market capitalisation exceed $4 trillion at any point in 2025?". This market belongs to the macro category. The market was triggered by NVIDIA Q1 2025 earnings report data center revenue on 2025-05-28. Odds moved from 0.29 to 0.64 (a +120.7% change). The market resolved YES on 2025-06-10. NVIDIA reported Q1 2025 data center revenue of $39 billion, beating estimates by 18%. The results pushed NVIDIA's market cap past $4 trillion for the first time on June 10, 2025. Continued sovereign AI infrastructure spending from governments globally drove the earnings beat.

Prediction market MA-003 asks: "Will US CPI inflation fall below 2.5% year-over-year in any month of 2026?". This market belongs to the macro category. The market was triggered by US May 2026 CPI inflation report on 2026-06-12. Odds moved from 0.41 to 0.67 (a +63.4% change). The market outcome is currently pending. The May 2026 CPI report showed headline inflation at 2.7%, the lowest reading since early 2024. Shelter costs finally began to cool, pulling overall inflation down. The Federal Reserve is watching closely as a sub-2.5% reading would support rate cuts at the June 2026 FOMC meeting.

Prediction market GE-001 asks: "Will the US impose additional tariffs of at least 10% on Chinese imports in the first half of 2025?". This market belongs to the macro category. The market was triggered by US-China trade policy tariff announcement January 2025 on 2025-01-22. Odds moved from 0.66 to 0.9 (a +36.4% change). The market resolved YES on 2025-02-04. President Trump signed an executive order imposing a 10% across-the-board tariff on all Chinese imports effective February 4, 2025. China retaliated with targeted tariffs on US agricultural exports. The move triggered a brief equity market sell-off and pushed up US consumer goods inflation expectations.

Prediction market GE-002 asks: "Will a formal ceasefire agreement between Russia and Ukraine be signed before July 1, 2026?". This market belongs to the macro category. The market was triggered by Russia-Ukraine peace negotiations US-mediated ceasefire talks 2026 on 2026-05-20. Odds moved from 0.21 to 0.38 (a +81.0% change). The market outcome is currently pending. US-mediated peace talks resumed in May 2026 with both Russia and Ukraine agreeing to a framework discussion. Prediction markets moved sharply on reports that a temporary ceasefire along current front lines was being considered. A formal signed agreement remains uncertain with the deadline under two weeks away.
